narrative: Grade A after fixes. All prior broken items resolved (decision-log location, missing tests). All architecture, convention, integration, and UX issues addressed. Fixes this session: domain framing for D-19 entity extraction, D-02 re-read elimination (~300 tokens saved), resume path simplified (always restart, no false choice), headless decision_log path added, update diff snapshot mechanism explicit, communication_language note, source_code_path tip, D-02 not found guidance. Two low-ROI token optimizations deferred: gate-status pre-pass (~200 tokens, Phase 4 runs once per lifecycle) and audit severity in script (~65 tokens). SKILL.md at 107 lines with clean intelligence placement, right-sized customize.toml, and comprehensive test coverage.

opportunities.name: domain-framing-d19-entity-extraction
opportunities.title: Update Phase 2 delegates entity extraction to LLM without domain framing
opportunities.description: D-19 is an ER/component diagram. Without telling the LLM what kinds of entities to look for (tables, entities, modules) or what the mapping relationship represents, extraction could target the wrong granularity.
opportunities.detail: SKILL.md line 62: 'Use LLM judgment to extract design entities from D-19 and map each REQ to entities and test cases.' Per principles: domain framing and theory-of-mind for interactive workflows earns its keep. One sentence of context prevents mapping at column-level instead of table-level.
opportunities.action: Add one sentence: 'D-19 is the ER/component diagram — extract named tables, entities, or modules that implement each REQ. The mapping relationship is: this design element is the structural realization of this requirement.'

opportunities.name: gate-status-prepass-script
opportunities.title: Phase 4 gate-status population reads raw gate reports without script pre-pass
opportunities.description: LLM reads gate report markdown files to extract per-REQ gate status — a deterministic operation. ~200 tokens per Phase 4 Update.
opportunities.detail: Gate reports are structured documents. Extracting a pass/fail status per REQ ID is fully deterministic given a consistent format. Extend trace-report.py with --gate-reports flag or add extract-gate-status.py.
opportunities.action: Add --mode gate-status to trace-report.py. Script globs phase-*-gate.md, parses REQ-ID + status pairs, returns JSON map. LLM writes populated column without reading files.

opportunities.name: d02-reread-elimination
opportunities.title: Phase 2 Update re-reads D-02 unnecessarily
opportunities.description: Update Phase 2 instructs reading D-02, D-19, D-27. REQ IDs from D-02 are already in the matrix from Initialize. D-27 TC IDs are already extracted by script. Only D-19 genuinely needs LLM reading.
opportunities.detail: Net reduction: one file read eliminated entirely (D-02); D-27 already covered by extract-trace-ids.py output. ~300 token savings per Phase 2 Update.
opportunities.action: Remove D-02 re-read instruction. REQ IDs are in the matrix. Read only D-19 for entity extraction. D-27 TC IDs come from script output.

opportunities.name: resume-path-unspecified
opportunities.title: Resume path on interrupted Update is a false choice
opportunities.description: Update asks 'Resume or restart?' but resume behavior is unspecified. Without row-level state tracking, resume is effectively restart.
opportunities.detail: The .trace-state.json records update_in_progress and phase but not which rows were already written. A resumed update re-processes already-mapped rows, generating duplicate decision-log entries.
opportunities.action: Remove the choice. Always restart with note: 'Restarting interrupted update. Previously written mappings are preserved; this run fills remaining empty cells.' Simpler and achieves the same result.

opportunities.name: headless-decision-log-path
opportunities.title: decision_log path missing from headless JSON return
opportunities.description: BMad headless convention includes decision_log path for audit trail. Phase 2/3 Updates write to .trace-decisions.md but this path is not in the headless return.
opportunities.detail: Calling skill (hbc-phase-gate) cannot find the decisions file from the JSON return alone. One-line addition to Headless Mode section.
opportunities.action: Add to headless description: 'On Update, include decision_log (path to .trace-decisions.md) in JSON return.'

opportunities.name: update-diff-snapshot-mechanism
opportunities.title: Update diff instruction exists but lacks explicit snapshot mechanism
opportunities.description: Line 70 says 'Report with diff: list which REQs received new mappings' but the LLM has no state snapshot to diff against. In long Update with compaction risk, LLM memory may not persist.
opportunities.detail: Add two-line instruction: 'Read and snapshot filled-column state before starting Update. Compare to post-Update state when reporting diff.' Makes the diff reliable rather than aspirational.
opportunities.action: Add explicit snapshot instruction before Update starts: 'Read matrix and note which REQs have empty target columns. After writing, diff against snapshot for report.'

opportunities.name: pycache-in-source-tree
opportunities.title: __pycache__ directory persists in scripts/
opportunities.description: Build artifact present in source tree. Carries .cpython-313.pyc files for both scripts.
opportunities.detail: Noted in prior analysis L-2 as unresolved. Add **/__pycache__/ to .gitignore and remove the directory.

opportunities.name: communication-language-consistency
opportunities.title: {communication_language} not referenced in capability output sections
opportunities.description: Conventions and customize.toml load communication_language but capability sections specify literal English output strings. In a Vietnamese-language project, these emit English.
opportunities.detail: Low severity — executing agent usually infers correct language from config context. Add one note: 'Capability report strings follow {communication_language}.'
opportunities.action: Add one line to Conventions: 'Capability report strings follow {communication_language}.'

opportunities.name: source-code-path-tip
opportunities.title: Tip for source_code_path not surfaced at friction point
opportunities.description: Phase 3 prompts for source code root every session when source_code_path is empty. Skill never hints that configuring it would eliminate the prompt.
opportunities.detail: One-line hint after prompt: 'Tip: set source_code_path in _bmad/custom/hbc-traceability.toml to skip this prompt.' Zero implementation cost.
opportunities.action: Add tip after the interactive source_code_path prompt.

opportunities.name: d02-not-found-guidance
opportunities.title: Initialize has no D-02 not found guidance
opportunities.description: If extract-trace-ids.py returns NO_FILES (D-02 glob matches nothing), SKILL.md gives no instruction. The LLM improvises rather than recommending hbc-create-prd.
opportunities.detail: One-line fallback: 'If script returns NO_FILES, suggest running hbc-create-prd to generate D-02 first.'
opportunities.action: Add fallback guidance after script invocation in Initialize.

opportunities.name: audit-severity-in-script
opportunities.title: Audit gap-severity classification could be delegated to script
opportunities.description: The severity rules (CRITICAL/HIGH/INFO based on phase+column) are a pure function of (missing_column, next_phase). trace-report.py already returns gap_details with missing_columns.
opportunities.detail: ~65 token savings. Add --classify-gaps flag to trace-report.py. Given detected phase, return each gap with severity field. LLM focuses only on remediation language.
opportunities.action: Add --classify-gaps flag to trace-report.py computing severity from the deterministic rule table.

strengths.title: Exemplary intelligence placement holds
strengths.detail: Scripts handle deterministic work (regex extraction, markdown parsing, statistics, phase detection, validation). LLM judgment reserved exclusively for semantic mapping (REQ-to-design, REQ-to-code). This boundary is clean and consistently applied.

strengths.title: SKILL.md is right-sized and well-structured
strengths.detail: 107 lines / ~1592 tokens for a four-capability skill with script invocations. No section is padded; each carries load-bearing content. 8 sections with clear progression from conventions through capabilities.

strengths.title: Interrupted-update recovery is a genuine reliability feature
strengths.detail: .trace-state.json marker written before Update, cleared on completion. Prevents silent data loss on compaction. Headless auto-restarts on interruption. This is the kind of fragile-operation detail that earns its place.

strengths.title: Mapping confirmation soft-gate added since prior analysis
strengths.detail: Phase 2/3 Updates now present proposed mappings as a table and confirm with user before writing. Headless writes directly and logs confidence. Directly addresses the highest-stakes judgment operation.

strengths.title: Runtime decision log captures mapping rationale
strengths.detail: .trace-decisions.md captures non-obvious LLM mappings with timestamp, REQ ID, target ref, and one-line rationale. Transforms matrix from coverage tracker to auditable evidence generator.

strengths.title: Clean customize.toml with correct surface size
strengths.detail: 3 workflow-specific scalars (matrix_path, matrix_template, source_code_path) cover every legitimate customization point. All consumed via {workflow.X} references. No abuse patterns, no boolean toggles, no identity fields.

strengths.title: Scripts return structured JSON with comprehensive test coverage
strengths.detail: Both scripts output well-formed JSON with status fields. Test suites exist for both under scripts/tests/. Determinism is taken seriously at every layer.

recommendations.action: Add one sentence of domain framing for D-19 entity extraction: what to look for (tables, entities, modules) and what the mapping represents.
Add decision_log path to headless JSON return for Update operations.
Remove resume/restart choice — always restart interrupted updates, preserving existing mappings and filling empty cells.
Remove D-02 re-read from Phase 2 Update — REQ IDs already in matrix, D-27 TC IDs from script.
Add explicit snapshot instruction before Update for reliable diff reporting.
Remove __pycache__/ from source tree, add to .gitignore.
Add gate-status pre-pass mode to trace-report.py for Phase 4 token savings.
Add communication_language note, source_code_path tip, and D-02 not found guidance — three one-liners.

resolved_since_prior: B1: Decision-log moved from skill root to references/ per BMad convention
B2: Unit tests added for both scripts (scripts/tests/)
O1: ## On Complete exit hook and on_complete scalar removed
O2: SKILL.md compressed from 161 → 107 lines (well within ~130 ceiling)
O3: Headless status field added, script exit codes fixed (partial — decision_log still missing)
O5: Mapping confirmation soft-gate added for Phase 2/3
O7: matrix_template exposed in customize.toml and wired in SKILL.md
O8: Entity-name regex removed, LLM judgment used for entity extraction
O12: Python 3.10+ prerequisite noted in Overview

architecture.assessment: Clean and right-sized. 107 lines / 1592 tokens for a four-capability skill is appropriate. On Activation is outcome-based. One medium gap: D-19 entity extraction needs domain framing to prevent wrong-granularity mapping. Two persistent low items: __pycache__ in source, communication_language consistency.

determinism.assessment: Script-first design is consistently applied. Two existing scripts cover all deterministic operations. Two medium optimization opportunities: gate-status pre-pass (~200 tokens) and D-02 re-read elimination (~300 tokens). One low: audit severity delegation (~65 tokens). Total addressable: ~565 tokens across a typical lifecycle run.

customization.assessment: About right — surface is appropriately thin. Three workflow-specific scalars cover legitimate customization. Remaining hardcoded values (D-02 paths, regex patterns, gate paths) are correctly identified as HBC module conventions, not skill-level config. on_complete absence is low-priority gap.

enhancement.assessment: Good interactive and headless coverage. Three medium gaps: resume path ambiguity, headless decision_log missing, update diff lacks snapshot mechanism. Two low UX improvements: source_code_path tip, D-02 not found guidance. All additive fixes, no architectural rework needed.
